project.name: memory-palaces
project.description: Mindscape (Your Memory Palace) is a phone-first, client-only React single-page app that turns the method of loci into a guided, repeatable memory-training practice: users build mental landscapes, place items along them, and train recall through spaced review and quizzes with progress tracked locally. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: SettingsScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level settings surface that renders the grouped settings list, animated scroll header, theme/notification toggles, and routes into all sub-screens (profile, password, phone, privacy, help, about, clear data). Orchestrates logout, language selection, and progress import/export.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses framer-motion useScroll/useTransform for a collapsing header and lazy skeleton loading state.

nodes.name: SettingsScreen
nodes.summary: Main settings component managing dark-mode/notification toggles, screen routing state, logout dialog, and progress import/export/backup-restore via ProgressUtils.

nodes.name: ProgressHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Profile/progress header showing avatar, username, animated XP counter with floating XP gain animation, level progress bar, and notification bell.
nodes.languageNotes: Animates XP count via setInterval-driven incremental tween with a floating XP overlay.

nodes.name: ProgressHeader
nodes.summary: Renders the gamified progress header and drives the animated XP roll-up and transient floating XP-gain indicator.

nodes.name: AboutScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Static About screen presenting app stats, version/build metadata, and external links (privacy, terms, website) with a back action.

nodes.name: AboutScreen
nodes.summary: Renders app information sections (stats, version metadata, external links) and opens links via window.open.

nodes.name: ChangePasswordScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Change-password form with live password-strength requirement checks (uppercase, lowercase, number, special char), show/hide toggles, and a simulated save.

nodes.name: ChangePasswordScreen
nodes.summary: Manages password input state, evaluates regex-based strength requirements, and simulates a save before invoking onPasswordChanged.

nodes.name: ClearDataScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Data-management screen letting users select categories of stored data to clear, showing storage usage, with a destructive confirmation dialog backed by ProgressUtils.

nodes.name: ClearDataScreen
nodes.summary: Manages selectable data-clearing options, formats storage byte sizes, and clears progress via ProgressUtils behind an alert-dialog confirmation.

nodes.name: formatBytes
nodes.summary: Formats a byte count into a human-readable string (KB/MB) using logarithmic scaling.

nodes.name: EditProfileScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Profile editing form for name, email, and bio with an avatar preview and a simulated save that calls onSave then navigates back.

nodes.name: EditProfileScreen
nodes.summary: Manages editable profile field state and simulates persistence before invoking onSave and onBack.

nodes.name: HelpCenterScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Help center presenting contact options (email/chat) and an accordion of FAQ categories and items.

nodes.name: HelpCenterScreen
nodes.summary: Renders contact options and an accordion-driven FAQ, opening external contact channels via window.open.

nodes.name: PhoneConnectScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Two-step phone connection flow: enter and format a phone number, then verify a one-time code, with simulated send/resend/verify and inline error handling.

nodes.name: PhoneConnectScreen
nodes.summary: Drives the phone-number entry and code-verification steps, formatting input and simulating send/verify before invoking onPhoneConnected.

nodes.name: formatPhoneNumber
nodes.summary: Strips non-digits and formats a raw phone string into grouped segments.

nodes.name: PrivacySettingsScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Privacy settings screen rendering a list of togglable privacy preferences backed by local state and Switch controls.

nodes.name: PrivacySettingsScreen
nodes.summary: Holds privacy preference toggle state and renders each setting with a Switch.

nodes.name: Checkbox.tsx
nodes.summary: Custom controlled checkbox primitive with optional label and themed checked/unchecked styling via cn.

nodes.name: Checkbox
nodes.summary: Renders a controlled checkbox with label, toggling via the onChange callback.

nodes.name: ImageWithFallback.tsx
nodes.summary: Image wrapper that swaps to a placeholder graphic when the source fails to load.

nodes.name: ImageWithFallback
nodes.summary: Renders an img and, on error, replaces it with a fallback placeholder image.

nodes.name: accordion.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn-style accordion primitives (Accordion, Item, Trigger, Content) wrapping Radix with themed styling.

nodes.name: alert-dialog.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn-style alert-dialog primitive set (overlay, content, header/footer, title/description, action/cancel, media) for destructive confirmation flows.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn Button component with class-variance-authority variants and the exported buttonVariants helper.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: Renders a themed button applying cva-derived variant and size classes via buttonVariants and cn.

nodes.name: command.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn command-palette primitives (Command, Dialog, Input, List, Group, Item, etc.) built on cmdk for searchable command menus.

nodes.name: dialog.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn-style dialog primitive set (overlay, content, header/footer, title/description, close) wrapping Radix Dialog.

nodes.name: input-group.tsx
nodes.summary: Composable input-group primitives (addon, button, text, input, textarea) with cva alignment variants for building rich input fields.

nodes.name: scroll-area.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn ScrollArea and ScrollBar primitives wrapping Radix scroll-area with themed scrollbars.

nodes.name: select.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn-style select primitive set (trigger, content, item, label, separator, scroll buttons) wrapping Radix Select.

nodes.name: switch.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn Switch toggle primitive with size variants wrapping Radix Switch.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the cn class-name merge helper (clsx + tailwind-merge) shared by all shadcn UI primitives.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges conditional class names with clsx and resolves Tailwind conflicts via tailwind-merge.

nodes.name: progressUtils.ts
nodes.summary: localStorage-backed progress persistence helper exposing export/import/clear/restore and storage-info operations with automatic backup on destructive writes.
nodes.languageNotes: Object-literal namespace pattern; uses Blob/URL for download and FileReader for import.

nodes.name: ProgressUtils
nodes.summary: Namespace object grouping exportProgress, importProgress, clearProgress, restoreFromBackup, and getStorageInfo over localStorage with backup safety.

nodes.name: HomePage.tsx
nodes.summary: Main authenticated app shell and navigation state machine that switches between home/palaces/profile tabs and overlay screens (palace detail, room training, quiz, create/edit) using React state instead of a router.
nodes.languageNotes: Router-less SPA navigation driven entirely by useState; orchestrates progress events into XP animations, notifications, and save indicators.

nodes.name: HomePage
nodes.summary: Root component that wires the central progress store to navigation, dispatching ProgressEvents to notifications and rendering tab content plus overlay screens.

nodes.name: ProfilePage.tsx
nodes.summary: Profile tab showing the user's XP, level, palace and room stats, and achievements, with a parallax scroll header driven by Framer Motion transforms.

nodes.name: ProfilePage
nodes.summary: Renders the profile view with scroll-linked animated header, statistics tab, and achievements derived from the central progress state.

nodes.name: SearchPopup.tsx
nodes.summary: Command-palette style search overlay for filtering palaces by query and category and selecting one to open.

nodes.name: SearchPopup
nodes.summary: Filters the palace list by search text and category and invokes the selection callback on choice.

nodes.name: ProgressNotification.tsx
nodes.summary: Toast-style notification card that renders type-specific colors and icons for progress events such as XP gains, level-ups, and achievements.

nodes.name: ProgressNotification
nodes.summary: Presentational notification component computing color/icon variants per notification type and rendering an auto-dismissable card.

nodes.name: SaveIndicator.tsx
nodes.summary: Small status badge that displays saving/saved/error feedback with matching colors, icons, and labels.

nodes.name: SaveIndicator
nodes.summary: Renders a save-status indicator with variant-specific styling derived from the current status.

nodes.name: CreatePalaceScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Multi-step palace creation wizard using react-hook-form with zod validation to collect name, category, icon, and color before creating a palace in the central store.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-hook-form + zodResolver with per-step trigger() validation gating navigation.

nodes.name: CreatePalaceScreen
nodes.summary: Three-step form wizard validating each step and calling createPalace on submit.

nodes.name: PalaceDetailScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Largest screen in the app: shows a palace's floors and rooms with swipeable room cards, inline add/edit/delete dialogs for floors and rooms, and progress summaries.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds a gesture-driven SwipeableRoomCard using @use-gesture useDrag and Framer Motion motion values; heavy inline CRUD for floors and rooms against the central store.

nodes.name: PalaceDetailScreen
nodes.summary: Renders palace floors/rooms and manages all floor and room CRUD flows, delete confirmations, and progress computation.

nodes.name: SwipeableRoomCard
nodes.summary: Draggable room card that reveals edit/delete actions on swipe using gesture binding and animated motion values.

nodes.name: getCurrentRoom
nodes.summary: Computes the next incomplete room across a palace's floors to highlight as the current training target.

nodes.name: RoomTrainingScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Flashcard-based room training experience with flip cards, like/dislike/save actions, and a success flow that records a training day and awards XP on completion.

nodes.name: RoomTrainingScreen
nodes.summary: Drives the flashcard training session, tracking card index/flip state and recording progress on completion.

nodes.name: ProgressDebugPanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Developer debug panel exposing actions to add XP, record training days, and reset progress for manual testing of the central store.

nodes.name: ProgressDebugPanel
nodes.summary: Renders debug buttons that invoke progress-store actions for testing XP, streaks, and reset.

nodes.name: TrainingCalendar.tsx
nodes.summary: Monthly calendar grid that highlights recorded training days from the progress state and supports navigating between months.

nodes.name: TrainingCalendar
nodes.summary: Builds a month grid with memoized training-day lookups and renders navigable calendar cells marking trained days.

nodes.name: TrainingStreak.tsx
nodes.summary: Visual streak indicator rendering a row of day markers reflecting the current consecutive training-day count.

nodes.name: TrainingStreak
nodes.summary: Renders a streak row of day markers based on the provided streak count.

nodes.name: PalaceQuizCompletionScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Quiz results screen showing score, performance level, and elapsed time with a confetti celebration and retake/next-palace actions.

nodes.name: PalaceQuizCompletionScreen
nodes.summary: Displays quiz outcome with performance level, formatted time, confetti, and navigation callbacks.

nodes.name: PalaceQuizScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Timed multiple-choice quiz over a palace's content, tracking score and answer feedback, awarding XP per correct answer, and recording a training day on completion.

nodes.name: PalaceQuizScreen
nodes.summary: Runs the timed quiz flow: question navigation, answer submission, scoring, XP awards, and completion reporting.

nodes.name: generateQuizQuestions
nodes.summary: Builds the multiple-choice question set for a palace from its rooms and content.

nodes.name: RiveAnimation.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the Rive runtime that loads an animation, wires state-machine inputs, and fires trigger/boolean inputs from props.

nodes.name: RiveAnimation
nodes.summary: Renders a Rive canvas and syncs trigger/boolean state-machine inputs to component props via effects.

nodes.name: useLocalStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Persistence primitive hook that mirrors a state value to localStorage and syncs across tabs via the storage event.

nodes.name: useLocalStorage
nodes.summary: Generic hook returning a state value backed by localStorage with cross-tab storage-event synchronization.

nodes.name: useNotifications.ts
nodes.summary: Notification queue hook that serializes progress notifications, displaying them one at a time with auto-dismiss timers.

nodes.name: useNotifications
nodes.summary: Manages a queue of notifications, processing them sequentially with show/hide callbacks and timeouts.

nodes.name: useProgressState.ts
nodes.summary: Central app store hook owning palaces, XP, level, streak, and notifications; defines core domain types (Palace/Floor/Room/ProgressState), persists to localStorage, and emits ProgressEvents for all CRUD and progress actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Single source of truth for the app: defines domain types and exposes a large actions surface (createPalace/Floor/Room, update/delete, addXP, completeRoom, recordTrainingDay) over a localStorage-backed state object.

nodes.name: useProgressState
nodes.summary: Central state hook exposing palace/floor/room CRUD and XP/streak actions over localStorage-persisted state, emitting ProgressEvents via an onEvent callback.

nodes.name: useSaveStatus.ts
nodes.summary: Hook managing transient save-status UI (saving/saved/error) with timed transitions, plus a calculateLevel helper deriving level from XP.

nodes.name: useSaveStatus
nodes.summary: Exposes triggerSave/triggerError actions that drive a transient save-status indicator with auto-clearing timers.

nodes.name: calculateLevel
nodes.summary: Derives the user's level from total XP using a fixed progression formula.

nodes.name: AmbientParticles.tsx
nodes.summary: Decorative component rendering a fixed set of randomly positioned, animated floating particles used as an ambient visual layer behind screens.

nodes.name: AmbientParticles
nodes.summary: Generates an array of particles with randomized position, size and timing via Math.random and maps them to animated absolutely-positioned elements.

nodes.name: DynamicBackground.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a layered gradient/dynamic background used as the base visual backdrop for the app's overlay and management screens.

nodes.name: DynamicBackground
nodes.summary: Renders the layered gradient background markup for screens, with no props or runtime state.

nodes.name: CreateFloorScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Overlay screen with a react-hook-form + zod validated form for creating a new floor inside a palace, persisting via the useProgressState actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-hook-form with a zodResolver for schema-based form validation.

nodes.name: CreateFloorScreen
nodes.summary: Renders the floor-creation form; resolves the parent palace from progress state, registers title/description fields, and on submit calls actions.createFloor before invoking onSuccess.

nodes.name: CreateRoomScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Overlay screen with a validated form for creating a new room within a floor of a palace, including content/title/description fields persisted via useProgressState actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses react-hook-form watch/setValue plus a zodResolver for validation.

nodes.name: CreateRoomScreen
nodes.summary: Renders the room-creation form scoped to a palace and floor; registers fields, watches values, and on submit calls actions.createRoom then onSuccess.

nodes.name: EditPalaceScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Overlay screen for editing an existing palace's name, description, category, icon and color with manual validation, persisting via actions.updatePalace.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses manual useState-based form state and a hand-written validateForm rather than react-hook-form.

nodes.name: EditPalaceScreen
nodes.summary: Loads the target palace into local form state via useEffect, validates name/description, and on submit calls actions.updatePalace with selected category/icon/color before onSuccess.

nodes.name: PalaceManagementScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Overlay screen listing all palaces with search and category filtering, expandable cards, and entry points to create, edit, delete palaces or manage their rooms.

nodes.name: PalaceManagementScreen
nodes.summary: Filters palaces by search query and category, renders expandable palace cards, and wires navigation callbacks plus a delete-confirmation flow calling actions.deletePalace.

nodes.name: RoomManagementScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Overlay screen for a single palace showing its floors and rooms with expand/collapse, completion progress, and create/edit/delete actions for floors and rooms.

nodes.name: RoomManagementScreen
nodes.summary: Resolves a palace from progress state, renders its floors with expandable room lists and computed progress, and wires create/edit callbacks plus delete-confirmation flows for floors and rooms.

nodes.name: PalaceProgressCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Home-screen card that animates an overall training-progress indicator and adapts its messaging and CTA depending on whether the user has any palaces yet.

nodes.name: PalaceProgressCard
nodes.summary: Animates displayed progress toward the target value via a setInterval tween in useEffect, selects a progress message, and renders a start-training or create-palace call to action.

nodes.name: StatusBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper rendering an iPhone-style status bar graphic with light/dark theme and configurable text color.

nodes.name: StatusBar
nodes.summary: Renders the imported StatusBarIPhone graphic with theme and textColor props applied.

nodes.name: input.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn-style Input primitive that merges base styling with consumer classes via the cn utility and forwards native input props.

nodes.name: Input
nodes.summary: Styled input element merging className via cn and spreading remaining native input props.

nodes.name: textarea.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn-style Textarea primitive that merges base styling with consumer classes via the cn utility and forwards native textarea props.

nodes.name: Textarea
nodes.summary: Styled textarea element merging className via cn and spreading remaining native textarea props.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel module re-exporting generated assets ProgressIllustration and StatusBarIPhone for consumption by UI components.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level auth/onboarding state machine that switches between opening, login, signup, success, and home screens based on a currentScreen state and an isAuthenticated flag persisted to localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Screen routing is done by a useState string switch rather than a router library, consistent with the project's no-router SPA design.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root component holding screen and authentication state; defines sign-in, sign-up, opening-complete, and welcome-complete handlers that mutate localStorage and advance the onboarding flow.

nodes.name: LoginScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen login UI with email/password fields, validation, show-password and remember-me toggles, and a simulated async sign-in that invokes the onSignIn callback after a timeout.

nodes.name: LoginScreen
nodes.summary: Login screen component managing email, password, loading, and toggle state; validates input and calls onSignIn / onSignup / onClose callbacks supplied by the parent App.

nodes.name: OpeningAnimation.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated splash sequence that progresses through timed phases of particle and orbiting effects before calling onAnimationComplete to advance the onboarding flow.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses staggered setTimeout phases cleaned up via a timers array in useEffect, and procedurally generated particles via Array map with Math.random/Math.cos/Math.sin.

nodes.name: OpeningAnimation
nodes.summary: Opening animation component driving a multi-phase intro with procedurally generated particles and orbiting elements, signalling completion via the onAnimationComplete callback.

nodes.name: SignupScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen registration UI with name/email/password fields, password-strength indicator, terms agreement, and a simulated async register that invokes the onSignUp callback.

nodes.name: SignupScreen
nodes.summary: Signup screen component managing form state and password-strength UI; validates input and calls onSignUp / onLogin / onClose callbacks from the parent App.

nodes.name: WelcomeSuccessScreen.tsx
nodes.summary: Celebratory post-signup screen that runs a phased confetti/trophy animation and feature highlights before calling onComplete to enter the authenticated home experience.
nodes.languageNotes: Phased setTimeout animation with cleanup, plus procedurally generated confetti and orbiting icons using Array map and Math trig/random.

nodes.name: WelcomeSuccessScreen
nodes.summary: Welcome success component animating a celebratory sequence and feature list, then invoking the onComplete callback to finish onboarding.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file re-exporting the shared auth building blocks AuthBackground, AuthInput, and SocialLogin for consumption by the login and signup screens.

nodes.name: SmoothScrollProvider.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight provider component that wraps the app to enable smooth scrolling behavior for its children.

nodes.name: SmoothScrollProvider
nodes.summary: Provider component that renders its children within smooth-scroll context.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: UI barrel file re-exporting the shared AppIcon, Checkbox, Divider, StatusBar components and the cn class-name helper for use across screens.

nodes.name: sonner.tsx
nodes.summary: Wrapper around the Sonner toast library exposing a preconfigured Toaster component for app-wide toast notifications.

nodes.name: Toaster
nodes.summary: Preconfigured Sonner Toaster component applying themed styling and default props for app notifications.

nodes.name: tooltip.tsx
nodes.summary: Radix-based tooltip primitives (Provider, Tooltip, Trigger, Content) with themed styling via the cn helper, reused across the UI.

nodes.name: TooltipProvider
nodes.summary: Tooltip provider primitive configuring delay and context for nested tooltips.

nodes.name: TooltipContent
nodes.summary: Tooltip content primitive rendering positioned, themed tooltip body with class names composed via cn.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that mounts the App into the DOM wrapped in MotionConfig, TooltipProvider, SmoothScrollProvider, and the Toaster, and imports global styles.

nodes.name: PalacesPage.tsx
nodes.summary: The palaces tab surface: renders the searchable, filterable, sortable list of memory palaces with grid/list view modes, category and sort dropdown controls, loading skeletons, empty states, and a delete-confirmation dialog wired to create/edit/delete callbacks.
nodes.languageNotes: Large single-function React component holding local UI state (viewMode, sortBy, selectedCategory, showDeleteConfirm) via multiple useState hooks; derives filteredPalaces/sortedPalaces with filter+sort pipelines.

nodes.name: PalacesPage
nodes.summary: Top-level palaces-tab component that filters and sorts the palace collection, toggles grid/list rendering, exposes category and sort dropdowns, and drives palace click, create, edit, and delete interactions including a confirmation dialog.

nodes.name: FeaturedPalaceBanner.tsx
nodes.summary: Hero banner card highlighting a featured palace with title, subtitle, item count, optional background image and icon, and an explore call-to-action.

nodes.name: FeaturedPalaceBanner
nodes.summary: Presentational banner component rendering a featured palace with title, subtitle, count, background image, icon, and an explore action.

nodes.name: PalaceCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable palace card displaying name, description, icon, progress, room counts, difficulty, rating, locked and favorite states across default/compact variants, with click and favorite-toggle handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: Variant-driven presentational component with local favorite state mirrored from props via useState.

nodes.name: PalaceCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single palace card with progress, metadata, difficulty, rating, locked/favorite indicators and variant styling, handling click and favorite-toggle interactions.

nodes.name: PalaceCardGrid.tsx
nodes.summary: Grid layout wrapper that maps a list of palaces into PalaceCard instances with configurable variant and column count, forwarding click and favorite-toggle callbacks.

nodes.name: PalaceCardGrid
nodes.summary: Maps a palaces array into a responsive grid of PalaceCard components, forwarding per-card click and favorite-toggle handlers.

nodes.name: PalaceCardSkeleton.tsx
nodes.summary: Loading placeholder mimicking a PalaceCard's layout using Skeleton primitives while palace data loads.

nodes.name: PalacesOverview.tsx
nodes.summary: Home-tab summary surface that pulls palace progress from useProgressState and renders an overview of palaces (circular or card variant) with ambient decoration, loading skeletons, empty state, and create/click callbacks.
nodes.languageNotes: Consumes useProgressState hook; renders animated decorative elements and slices palaces for compact display.

nodes.name: PalacesOverview
nodes.summary: Renders a home-tab palaces overview from progress state, supporting circular and card variants, loading skeletons, an empty state, and palace-click/create handlers.

nodes.name: EmptyState.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable empty-state primitive rendering an icon or emoji, title, description, and optional action button via the cn class-merge helper.

nodes.name: EmptyState
nodes.summary: Presentational empty-state component composing an icon/emoji, title, description, and optional action.

nodes.name: Skeleton.tsx
nodes.summary: Base skeleton primitive rendering an animated placeholder block with merged className via the cn helper.

nodes.name: dropdown-menu.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui dropdown-menu wrapper exporting styled Radix dropdown primitives (trigger, content, item, checkbox/radio items, submenus, separators, shortcuts) built on the cn helper.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin styled wrappers over Radix UI dropdown primitives; many small forwarding components re-exported together.

nodes.name: DropdownMenuContent
nodes.summary: Styled Radix dropdown content surface with configurable alignment, side, and offsets, merging classes via cn.

nodes.name: DropdownMenuItem
nodes.summary: Styled Radix dropdown menu item supporting inset and default/destructive variants.

nodes.name: DropdownMenuSubTrigger
nodes.summary: Styled Radix submenu trigger with chevron indicator and inset support.

nodes.name: DropdownMenuCheckboxItem
nodes.summary: Styled Radix checkbox dropdown item rendering a checked indicator.

nodes.name: DropdownMenuRadioItem
nodes.summary: Styled Radix radio dropdown item rendering a selected indicator.

nodes.name: tabs.tsx
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui tabs wrapper exporting styled Radix Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, and TabsContent with a cva-based variants helper, built on the cn class-merge utility.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses class-variance-authority (tabsListVariants) for variant-driven styling over Radix Tabs primitives.

nodes.name: Tabs
nodes.summary: Styled Radix Tabs root component with orientation support and merged classes.

nodes.name: TabsList
nodes.summary: Styled Radix TabsList applying variant styling via tabsListVariants and cn.

nodes.name: LiquidGlassBottomNav.tsx
nodes.summary: Primary in-app bottom navigation bar with a liquid-glass aesthetic and a spring-animated active-tab indicator; renders home/palaces/profile tabs and invokes onTabChange.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Framer Motion (useMotionValue, useSpring) and useRef-based DOM measurement to position the sliding indicator under the active tab.

nodes.name: LiquidGlassBottomNav
nodes.summary: React component that measures tab button geometry and drives a spring-animated indicator to the active tab, calling onTabChange when a tab is pressed.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel module that re-exports navigation and status-bar icon components from NavIcons and StatusBarIcons.
nodes.languageNotes: TypeScript barrel using wildcard re-exports (export *).

nodes.name: StatusBarIPhone.tsx
nodes.summary: Imported iOS status-bar visual rendering the time and signal/wifi/battery level indicators with a configurable text color.

nodes.name: StatusBarIPhone
nodes.summary: Default-exported component composing the Time and Levels subcomponents into a full iPhone-style status bar.

nodes.name: Time
nodes.summary: Renders the status-bar clock text with a configurable text color.

nodes.name: Levels
nodes.summary: Renders the cellular signal, wifi, and battery level icons with a configurable color.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Repository guide for Claude Code describing the project, commands, stack, and architecture (state-machine navigation, localStorage persistence, component organization, and styling system).

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Full Mindscape design system specification covering colors, typography, elevation, and component styling rules (buttons, chips, cards, inputs, and the liquid-glass bottom navigation).

nodes.name: PRODUCT.md
nodes.summary: Product brief defining purpose, target users, brand personality, design principles, and accessibility commitments for the Mindscape memory trainer.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: shadcn/ui configuration (base-nova style, base-ui registry, RTL off) pointing Tailwind at src/styles/tailwind.css and defining import aliases under @/app/.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry document that defines the app root element and loads /src/main.tsx as the application bootstrap module.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm manifest for the memory-palaces package declaring Vite build/dev/preview scripts plus React runtime and tooling dependencies.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Tooling lockfile pinning the versions of installed agent skills used in the repository.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Primary TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode with the @/* path alias mapping to src/*, and referencing tsconfig.node.json for build tooling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses project references plus a paths alias (@/*) to drive module resolution for app source.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the Node-side build tooling (e.g. the Vite config), referenced as a project from the root tsconfig.json.

nodes.name: GENERATE_AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Meta-prompt instructing an AI coding agent to analyze the codebase and author a fresh AGENTS.md guide, emphasizing architecture, developer workflows, and project-specific conventions sourced from existing AI rule files.

nodes.name: UPDATE_AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Meta-prompt directing an AI coding agent to apply minimal, surgical updates to an existing AGENTS.md by diffing the codebase against the current baseline and preserving accurate sections.

nodes.name: role.md
nodes.summary: Persona definition for an AI agent acting as a senior engineer and UI/UX designer for the Memory Palace app, specifying zero-legacy, premium-design, and completeness execution rules.

nodes.name: fonts.css
nodes.summary: Loads the Lexend webfont (weights 300-700) from Google Fonts, providing the single typeface used across the entire app per the One Family Rule.
nodes.languageNotes: Single @import url() pulling Lexend with display=swap to avoid blocking text rendering.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: CSS entry point that imports the styling layers in order: fonts (Lexend), Tailwind v4 + shadcn base, then the design-token theme.
nodes.languageNotes: Order matters: fonts and Tailwind base load before theme.css so design tokens override the shadcn defaults.

nodes.name: tailwind.css
nodes.summary: Tailwind v4 setup configured entirely in-CSS via @theme/@custom-variant (no tailwind.config.js), wiring shadcn color variables, the Lexend font-sans utility, a radius scale, and base layer resets.
nodes.languageNotes: Tailwind v4 CSS-first config: @theme inline maps --color-* utilities to shadcn CSS variables, @custom-variant defines the dark variant, and @layer base applies global resets.

nodes.name: theme.css
nodes.summary: Design-token system defining the learning-app color palette, typography sizes, shadows, radii, and gradients as CSS custom properties, mapping them onto shadcn variables and exposing named .text-* utility classes that bake in colors.
nodes.languageNotes: Tokens flow through Tailwind compatibility variables; note that .text-* utilities hardcode colors and can override Tailwind text-[#...] arbitrary-value classes.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the `cn` className helper combining clsx and tailwind-merge, widely imported across the app for conditional styling.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard shadcn-style `cn` utility merging clsx output through tailwind-merge to dedupe conflicting Tailwind classes.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges conditional class values via clsx and resolves Tailwind conflicts via twMerge.

layers.name: Entry & Bootstrap
layers.description: Application bootstrap: the Vite HTML host, the React mount point in main.tsx, and App.tsx which drives the auth/onboarding-to-home screen state machine.

layers.name: App Shell & Navigation
layers.description: The authenticated shell and tab navigation (HomePage state machine, bottom nav, Home/Palaces/Profile/Settings surfaces) plus ambient backgrounds that frame every screen.

layers.name: Authentication & Onboarding
layers.description: Pre-auth onboarding flow and credential screens (opening animation, login, signup, welcome success) and their shared auth form/background building blocks.

layers.name: Palace & Training Feature
layers.description: The core method-of-loci experience: palace/floor/room CRUD screens, flashcard room training, timed quizzes, palace cards, and search results.

layers.name: Progress & Notifications
layers.description: Gamification surfaces - XP/level headers, training streak and calendar, palace progress cards, and the toast/inline notification and save-status indicators.

layers.name: UI Primitives
layers.description: Shared shadcn/Radix-based design-system primitives (button, dialog, select, switch, tooltip, command palette, etc.) and the cn class-merge helper consumed across all features.

layers.name: State & Persistence
layers.description: The central useProgressState store (domain types and CRUD/XP actions), supporting hooks (localStorage, notifications, save status), and the localStorage-backed progress export/import utilities.

layers.name: Styling & Visual Assets
layers.description: The layered CSS design-token system (fonts/tailwind/theme) plus icon sets, imported Rive/SVG visuals, and 3D category-icon components.

layers.name: Build & Configuration
layers.description: Project and build configuration: Vite config, TypeScript configs, shadcn components.json, package manifest, and ambient type declarations.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project and AI-agent process documentation (CLAUDE/DESIGN/PRODUCT briefs and docs/ai_docs meta-prompts).

tour.title: What Mindscape Is
tour.description: Start with the product and architecture briefs to understand the project before touching code. PRODUCT.md defines Mindscape as a phone-first memory trainer built around the method of loci, along with its brand voice and accessibility commitments, while CLAUDE.md describes the big-picture engineering shape: a client-only React SPA with no backend, no router, and all state persisted to localStorage. Read these together to form the mental model the rest of the tour fills in.

tour.title: Entry & Bootstrap
tour.description: index.html defines the single root element and loads src/main.tsx, which is the true application entry point. main.tsx mounts the App wrapped in four global providers: MotionConfig (with reducedMotion set to 'user' for accessibility), TooltipProvider, SmoothScrollProvider, and the Toaster, and it imports the global stylesheet. This is the outermost shell that every screen renders inside.
tour.languageLesson: React 18 boots via createRoot(...).render(). Wrapping the tree in context providers here makes cross-cutting concerns (motion preferences, tooltips, toasts) available everywhere without prop drilling.

tour.title: The Auth & Onboarding State Machine
tour.description: App.tsx is the first thing main.tsx renders, and it owns the pre-authenticated flow. Instead of a router, it holds a currentScreen string and an isAuthenticated flag in state (persisted to localStorage) and switches between the opening animation, login, signup, welcome-success, and the authenticated home experience. This sets the pattern you will see repeated throughout the app: navigation is just React state, not URLs.
tour.languageLesson: This is a router-less SPA: a useState string acts as a finite state machine. Each screen is a discrete state, and handlers advance the machine rather than calling navigate().

tour.title: The Central Store: useProgressState
tour.description: useProgressState is the heart of the app and its single source of truth. Building on the no-backend design from Step 1, this hook owns palaces, XP, level, and streak; defines the core domain types (Palace, Floor, Room, ProgressState); persists everything to localStorage; and emits ProgressEvents for every CRUD and progress action. Almost every feature screen reads and mutates app data through this one hook, which is why it has the highest fan-in in the entire codebase.
tour.languageLesson: A single large hook exposing a domain model plus an actions surface is a lightweight alternative to Redux/Zustand. The onEvent callback decouples 'something changed' from 'show a notification', keeping the store presentation-agnostic.

tour.title: Persistence & Supporting Hooks
tour.description: These hooks are the machinery beneath the store from Step 4. useLocalStorage is the persistence primitive that mirrors state to localStorage and syncs across browser tabs via the storage event; progressUtils layers export/import/clear/restore operations with automatic backup on destructive writes; and useSaveStatus and useNotifications drive the transient 'saving/saved' indicator and the serialized notification queue that consume the ProgressEvents the store emits.
tour.languageLesson: Listening to the window 'storage' event lets multiple tabs stay in sync with no backend. The store stays the source of truth while useLocalStorage handles serialization and cross-tab propagation.

tour.title: The App Shell & Navigation Hub
tour.description: Once authenticated, HomePage becomes the app shell and the second, larger state machine. It switches between the home, palaces, and profile tabs and layers in overlay screens (palace detail, room training, quiz, create/edit) entirely with React state, while wiring the central store's ProgressEvents into XP animations, notifications, and save indicators. The LiquidGlassBottomNav is the primary tab switcher, with a spring-animated active indicator that embodies the design system's signature liquid-glass aesthetic.
tour.languageLesson: HomePage has by far the highest fan-out in the app because it is the orchestration hub: it imports nearly every feature screen and composes them as conditional overlays rather than routes.

tour.title: Browsing Palaces
tour.description: PalacesPage is the palaces-tab surface and the primary entry into the core feature. It renders the searchable, filterable, sortable list of memory palaces with grid/list view modes, loading skeletons, empty states, and delete confirmation, delegating each item to the reusable PalaceCard. These connect directly to the Palace domain type from Step 4 and form the browsing layer of the method-of-loci experience.

tour.title: Building & Managing a Palace
tour.description: These screens are how users construct the spatial structure central to the method of loci. CreatePalaceScreen is a multi-step wizard (react-hook-form + zod) that adds a palace to the store, while PalaceDetailScreen is the largest screen in the app, rendering a palace's floors and rooms with swipeable cards and inline add/edit/delete dialogs. CreateFloorScreen, CreateRoomScreen, and EditPalaceScreen round out the CRUD, all persisting through the useProgressState actions from Step 4.
tour.languageLesson: Forms use react-hook-form with a zodResolver so validation rules live in a schema; the wizard gates step navigation by awaiting trigger() to validate only the current step's fields.

tour.title: Training & Quizzes
tour.description: This is where memorization actually happens. RoomTrainingScreen is a flashcard experience with flip cards and like/save actions that records a training day and awards XP on completion, while PalaceQuizScreen runs a timed multiple-choice quiz over a palace's content, scoring answers and granting XP, before PalaceQuizCompletionScreen celebrates the result with confetti. Each of these calls back into the store's addXP and recordTrainingDay actions, closing the loop with the gamification surfaces covered next.

tour.title: Progress, Streaks & Notifications
tour.description: These surfaces make the XP, level, and streak data from the store visible and rewarding. ProgressHeader shows the animated XP counter and level bar, TrainingStreak and TrainingCalendar visualize consecutive training days, and ProgressNotification renders the toast cards driven by the ProgressEvents the store emits (queued one at a time by useNotifications from Step 5). Together they implement the gamification feedback promised in the product brief.

tour.title: Settings & Data Ownership
tour.description: SettingsScreen is the top-level settings surface and routes into all its sub-screens (profile, password, phone, privacy, help, about), with a collapsing animated header. The most architecturally important stop here is ClearDataScreen, which uses progressUtils to export, import, and clear the user's locally stored progress, reinforcing the project's no-backend, user-owns-their-data design from Step 1.

tour.title: Shared UI Primitives
tour.description: Beneath every screen sits a library of shadcn/Radix-based primitives. button, dialog, select, switch, and input provide accessible, themeable building blocks, while utils.ts exports the cn helper (clsx + tailwind-merge) that merges class names for all of them. These have high fan-in because the entire feature layer composes from them, keeping interaction patterns and styling consistent across the app.
tour.languageLesson: cn() merges clsx conditionals and resolves conflicting Tailwind classes via tailwind-merge, so a consumer's className can safely override a primitive's defaults. Components use class-variance-authority (cva) to define typed style variants.

tour.title: The Styling & Design-Token System
tour.description: index.css is the styling entry point and its import order is load-bearing: fonts (the single Lexend family) first, then Tailwind v4 + the shadcn base, then the design-token theme. tailwind.css configures Tailwind v4 entirely in-CSS via @theme/@custom-variant with no config file, and theme.css defines the color palette, typography, shadows, and gradients as CSS custom properties that map onto shadcn variables. This realizes the DESIGN.md system from Step 1 in code.
tour.languageLesson: Tailwind v4 moves configuration into CSS: @theme declares design tokens and @custom-variant defines variants, eliminating tailwind.config.js. Import order matters because later layers depend on tokens defined earlier.

tour.title: Build & Configuration
tour.description: Close the tour with the project's orientation files. vite.config.ts wires the React and Tailwind plugins and the @-to-src path alias; tsconfig.json enables strict TypeScript with that same alias; components.json configures shadcn (base-nova style) and points it at the Tailwind stylesheet; and package.json declares the Vite dev/build/preview scripts. Together these define how the source you have just toured gets compiled, aliased, and served.
